solutions.solution: (e) Line rotates through 135°, 90°, 135°, 90°, respectively, in clockwise direction. One of the two symbols is shifted in place of the other symbol and a new symbol is formed at its place. The same process is repeated in the successive figures.

(c) Changes from figure (1) to (2), from (2) to (3) and from figure (3) to (4) take place as shown in the diagram. (•) represents the element which is replaced by a new element. The same process is repeated in the subsequent figures.

(b) From figure (1) to (2), one arc is inverted, from figure (2) to (3), one arc is inverted, then from figure (3) to (4), one arc is inverted and two arcs are inverted from figure (4) to (5). The same process is repeated from problem figure (5) to the answer figure.
(d) Elements moves from left to upper right → lower right → upper left → lower left-up path position in subsequent steps. A new element appears in each step in the left base.
(b) Change from figure (4) to figure (5) is the same as change from figure (1) to (2). So, change from figure (5) to (6) is the same as the change from figure (2) to (3).
(e) Changes in position of elements from figure (1) to (2) = from figure (3) to (4) = from figure (5) to (6). Such changes take place as below

(d) The central figure is same in figure (5) as it is in figure (2). Hence, it will be same in answer figure as it is in figure (3). The outer figure is same in figure (5) as it is in figure (1). Hence, it will be same in answer figure as it is in figure (2).

(e) Figures change in such a way that dot lies in the space common to circle and triangle in one step and it lies in the space common to triangle and rectangle in next step. This process is repeated in the subsequent figures.

(c) The direction of U is same in figure (1) and (4) and also in figure (2) and (5). So, the direction of U in the answer figure must be same as it is in figure (3). Also, N is inverted in each subsequent figure. For two subsequent figures, the direction of the arrow remains same and then changes to the other side and again remains same for two subsequent figures.

(e) From figure (1) to (2), first symbol, counting from left hand side occupies the position of second symbol and is inverted, second symbol comes at fourth position, fourth symbol comes at third position and is inverted and third symbol comes at first position and is replaced by a new one. Same changing pattern follows from figure (3) to (4). So, the same changes in figure (5) will result into the answer figure.
(d) Changes in positions of elements from figure 1 to 2 = from figure 3 to 4 = from figure 5 to 6. Let us see how the changes take place.
(b) For the upper part follow, if 1 = 5, then 2 = 6 pattern.
(c) In each step, two of the elements interchange positions while the remaining three shift one step clockwise in a cyclic order. This movement itself shifts in clockwise order.
(d) Changes take place from figure (1) to (2) and from figure (2) to (3) as shown in the diagram. The same process is repeated from figure (3) to (4) and from figure (4) to (5) and so on.

(c) In each of the successive figures, a new sign is formed at the top left position and the symbol it replaces comes in the middle. The sign at the middle position comes at the middle top position and the same process is repeated in the successive figures.

(e) In alternate steps, from (1) to (2), (3) to (4) and (5) to (6), the uppermost element goes to lowermost, the other elements shift one step upward and the element reaching the uppermost position is replaced by a new one.

(b) In each of the successive figures, a new outer design is formed. Arrow rotates anti-clockwise through angles of 45°, 90°, 135°, 180°, 225°, ... and the figure inside the main design starts getting doubled from figure (4) onwards.
(d) 'U' is revolving in clockwise direction in every step and also a new symbol 'U' is added in every step hence (d) is the answer.

(d) In the problem figures, there comes a circle and a square at the centre alternately. So, in the 7th figure, there must b a circle at the centre. Also, the direction of arrows is same in figure (1) and (4), (2) and (5), (3) and (6). So, the direction of arrows in figure (7) must be the same as in figure (4). So, option (d) is the right choice.
(b) In each step, the left most symbol gets shifted to the right most side and also gets inverted.

(b) From the four figures, we can say that the pattern of movement of the triangle is anti-clockwise one box at a time, along the outer edge, while the movement of the semi-circle like element is anti-clockwise along the inner four squares. Thus (b) represents the next of movements of the two elements proceeding from figure (4).
(c) The heads of the arrow move on the arrow line in a set order.

(d) From problem figure (1) to (2), the upper half of one arrow rotates 45° clockwise, from figure (2) to (3), the lower half of the same arrow rotates 45° clockwise. From figure (3) to (4) and also from (4) to (5), the same process is repeated for the other arrow.
